---
title: "Economics"
description: "GDP, inflation, and economic indicators — Canada and OECD peers"
---

```{python}
#| echo: false
import sys
sys.path.insert(0, "..")
import pandas as pd
import plotly.graph_objects as go
from pipeline.config import (
    CANADA_COLOR, PEER_COLOR, HIGHLIGHT_WIDTH, PEER_WIDTH,
    HIGHLIGHT_COUNTRY, PEER_COUNTRIES, DATA_DATE,
)
```

## Inflation (CPI Year-over-Year)

Canada's inflation rate, measured as the year-over-year change in the Consumer Price Index (all items). The Bank of Canada targets 2% inflation (shown as a dashed line).

```{python}
#| echo: false
try:
    cpi = pd.read_csv("../data/economics/statcan_cpi.csv", parse_dates=["date"])
    # Filter to single "All-items" series (not sub-categories)
    if "product_group" in cpi.columns:
        cpi = cpi[cpi["product_group"] == "All-items"]
    cpi = cpi.sort_values("date").reset_index(drop=True)
    # Recompute YoY in case CSV has stale values from mixed product groups
    cpi["cpi_value"] = pd.to_numeric(cpi["cpi_value"], errors="coerce")
    cpi["inflation_yoy"] = cpi["cpi_value"].pct_change(periods=12) * 100
    cpi = cpi.dropna(subset=["inflation_yoy"])

    # Color bars: red above target, blue below
    colors = [CANADA_COLOR if v >= 2 else "#1f77b4" for v in cpi["inflation_yoy"]]

    fig = go.Figure()
    fig.add_trace(go.Bar(
        x=cpi["date"],
        y=cpi["inflation_yoy"],
        marker_color=colors,
        hovertemplate="<b>%{x|%b %Y}</b><br>Inflation: %{y:.1f}%<extra></extra>",
    ))

    # Bank of Canada 2% target
    fig.add_hline(y=2, line_dash="dash", line_color="#1f77b4", opacity=0.7,
                  annotation_text="2% target", annotation_position="top left")

    fig.update_layout(
        title="Canada Inflation Rate (CPI Year-over-Year Change)",
        xaxis_title="",
        yaxis_title="Inflation (%)",
        plot_bgcolor="white",
        xaxis=dict(
            gridcolor="#e0e0e0",
            rangeselector=dict(
                buttons=[
                    dict(count=5, label="5Y", step="year", stepmode="backward"),
                    dict(count=10, label="10Y", step="year", stepmode="backward"),
                    dict(count=20, label="20Y", step="year", stepmode="backward"),
                    dict(step="all", label="All"),
                ],
                x=1, xanchor="right", y=1.15,
            ),
        ),
        yaxis=dict(gridcolor="#e0e0e0", autorange=True, fixedrange=False),
        showlegend=False,
        margin=dict(b=80),
        annotations=[dict(
            text=f"Source: Statistics Canada, Table 18-10-0004-01 | Data as of: {DATA_DATE}",
            xref="paper", yref="paper", x=0, y=-0.15,
            showarrow=False, font=dict(size=10, color="#999"),
        )],
    )
    fig.show()
except FileNotFoundError:
    print("CPI data not yet available. Run the data pipeline to fetch it.")
```

## GDP per Capita (PPP, Current USD)

GDP per capita at purchasing power parity allows meaningful comparison of living standards across countries. Canada is shown in red against OECD peers.

```{python}
#| echo: false
try:
    df = pd.read_csv("../data/economics/oecd_gdp_per_capita.csv")

    fig = go.Figure()

    # Peers first (grey)
    for code in sorted(df["country_code"].unique()):
        if code == HIGHLIGHT_COUNTRY:
            continue
        subset = df[df["country_code"] == code]
        name = PEER_COUNTRIES.get(code, code)
        fig.add_trace(go.Scatter(
            x=subset["year"],
            y=subset["gdp_per_capita"],
            name=name,
            mode="lines",
            line=dict(color=PEER_COLOR, width=PEER_WIDTH),
            opacity=0.5,
            hovertemplate=f"<b>{name}</b>: $%{{y:,.0f}}<extra></extra>",
        ))

    # Canada on top
    can = df[df["country_code"] == HIGHLIGHT_COUNTRY]
    fig.add_trace(go.Scatter(
        x=can["year"],
        y=can["gdp_per_capita"],
        name="Canada",
        mode="lines+markers",
        line=dict(color=CANADA_COLOR, width=HIGHLIGHT_WIDTH),
        marker=dict(size=5),
        hovertemplate="<b>Canada</b>: $%{y:,.0f}<extra></extra>",
    ))

    # Peer average
    avg = df.groupby("year")["gdp_per_capita"].mean().reset_index()
    fig.add_trace(go.Scatter(
        x=avg["year"],
        y=avg["gdp_per_capita"],
        name="Peer Average",
        mode="lines",
        line=dict(color="#1f77b4", width=2, dash="dash"),
        hovertemplate="<b>Peer Average</b>: $%{y:,.0f}<extra></extra>",
    ))

    fig.update_layout(
        title="GDP per Capita (PPP, Current USD)",
        xaxis_title="Year",
        yaxis_title="USD (PPP)",
        yaxis_tickformat="$,.0f",
        plot_bgcolor="white",
        xaxis=dict(
            gridcolor="#e0e0e0",
            rangeselector=dict(
                buttons=[
                    dict(count=10, label="10Y", step="year", stepmode="backward"),
                    dict(count=15, label="15Y", step="year", stepmode="backward"),
                    dict(step="all", label="All"),
                ],
                x=1, xanchor="right", y=1.15,
            ),
        ),
        yaxis=dict(gridcolor="#e0e0e0"),
        hovermode="x unified",
        legend=dict(
            orientation="h", yanchor="bottom", y=-0.3,
            xanchor="center", x=0.5,
            itemclick="toggle", itemdoubleclick="toggleothers",
        ),
        margin=dict(b=140),
        annotations=[dict(
            text=f"Source: OECD National Accounts | Data as of: {DATA_DATE}",
            xref="paper", yref="paper", x=0, y=-0.42,
            showarrow=False, font=dict(size=10, color="#999"),
        )],
    )
    fig.show()
except FileNotFoundError:
    print("GDP per capita data not yet available. Run the data pipeline to fetch it.")
```

## Latest GDP per Capita: Ranked

```{python}
#| echo: false
try:
    df = pd.read_csv("../data/economics/oecd_gdp_per_capita.csv")

    latest_year = df["year"].max()
    latest = df[df["year"] == latest_year].copy()
    latest["country_name"] = latest["country_code"].map(PEER_COUNTRIES)
    latest = latest.sort_values("gdp_per_capita", ascending=True)

    colors = [CANADA_COLOR if c == "CAN" else PEER_COLOR for c in latest["country_code"]]

    fig = go.Figure()
    fig.add_trace(go.Bar(
        x=latest["gdp_per_capita"],
        y=latest["country_name"],
        orientation="h",
        marker_color=colors,
        hovertemplate="%{y}: $%{x:,.0f}<extra></extra>",
    ))

    fig.update_layout(
        title=f"GDP per Capita, PPP ({latest_year})",
        xaxis_title="USD (PPP)",
        xaxis_tickformat="$,.0f",
        yaxis_title="",
        plot_bgcolor="white",
        xaxis=dict(gridcolor="#e0e0e0"),
        showlegend=False,
        height=600,
        annotations=[dict(
            text=f"Source: OECD National Accounts | Data as of: {DATA_DATE}",
            xref="paper", yref="paper", x=1, y=-0.1,
            showarrow=False, font=dict(size=10, color="#999"),
        )],
    )
    fig.show()
except FileNotFoundError:
    print("GDP per capita data not yet available. Run the data pipeline to fetch it.")
```

## Download Data

- [CPI / Inflation (CSV)](../data/economics/statcan_cpi.csv) — Statistics Canada Table 18-10-0004-01
- [GDP per Capita (CSV)](../data/economics/oecd_gdp_per_capita.csv) — OECD National Accounts, PPP current USD
